reasoning: The reference value as of late October 2025 is 1.000, which means there were zero ACLED-recorded 'Battles' in Jordan over the preceding 360 days. To meet the resolution criteria of more than 10 times this baseline, Jordan would need to experience at least 11 'Battles' (violent interactions between organized armed groups) within a single 30-day period leading up to February 2026. Given Jordan's robust internal security and the historical absence of such conflicts on its soil, experiencing 11 battles in one month would require an unprecedented black-swan escalation into severe civil unrest, an active insurgency, or conventional war. The probability of such a rapid deterioration from 0 battles to 11+ per month in just a few months is extremely low. Therefore, the minimum allowable probability estimate (0.02) is appropriate.
